title: Specialist IT Infrastructure - Cloud and Datacenter (m/w/x)
description:
Your responsibilities

- Ensuring reliable day-to-day operations of datacenter and cloud infrastructure environments
- Operating, monitoring, and maintaining hybrid infrastructure, including servers, virtualization platforms, storage, backup, and cloud services
- Managing and optimizing system performance, availability, and capacity
- Executing incident, problem, and change management processes in alignment with ITIL practices
- Providing 2nd and 3rd level support, including troubleshooting and root cause analysis of complex incidents
- Maintaining and operating Active Directory and Entra services (identity, authentication, policies)
- Implementing and maintaining backup and recovery solutions (e.g. Veeam) and ensuring disaster recovery readiness
- Collaborating closely with Architecture teams to implement new solutions and ensure smooth transitions into operations
- Ensuring compliance with IT security policies in coordination with the CISO
- Maintaining accurate and up-to-date technical documentation and operational procedures
- Monitoring infrastructure health and proactively identifying risks and improvement opportunities
- Coordinating with external providers and vendors for support and service delivery
- Supporting automation and continuous improvement of operational processes
Your profile

- Good command of the English language (reading, writing, speaking)
- Strong operational mindset with focus on stability, reliability, and service quality
- Hands-on experience in operating complex IT environments (virtualization, high availability, global setups)
- Solid experiens with Window and Linux Server, VMware vSphere or comparable virtualization platforms, Backup solutions, Cloud platforms, Active Directory
- Certifications fitting to the job are welcome
- Basic-to-intermediate knowledge in networking technologies
- Experience with monitoring tools and incident management processes
- Structured, solution-oriented working style with strong troubleshooting skills
- Strong service and customer orientation when working with internal users and external partners
- Team player with good self-organization and ability to work under pressure
- High degree of reliability, responsibility, and ownership
- Willingness to travel
DAS KANNST DU VON UNS ERWARTEN

- attraktives Gesundheitsprogramm
- interessante Aufgabe in globalem Unternehmen
- dynamisches und aufgeschlossenes Team
- flexible, familienfreundliche Arbeitszeiten
- vielfältige Weiterbildungsangebote
- viele Zusatz- und Bonusleistungen

KONTAKT:
Diese Stelle wird über ein VORAUSWAHLVERFAHREN des AMS Wels besetzt.

Wenn Sie an diesem Stellenangebot interessiert sind, senden Sie Ihre schriftlichen Bewerbungsunterlagen, unter Angabe der AUFTRAGSNUMMER *17212860* an:

E-Mail: sfu.wels_9@ams.at
oder an: Arbeitsmarktservice Wels, z.Hd. Fr. Mag. Anna Wallner, Rainerstraße 1, 4600 Wels

Wir leiten Ihre Unterlagen bei entsprechender Eignung sofort an unseren Auftraggeber weiter, der sich anschließend mit Ihnen in Verbindung setzt.

Im Sinne des GIBG wenden wir uns mit dieser Ausschreibung an Damen und Herren. Wir weisen darauf hin, dass wir etwaige Aufwendungen (z.B. Fahrtkosten, Tages- oder Nächtigungsgelder) im Zuge Ihrer Bewerbung nicht ersetzen.
